title: First Class: Potions
prose: The dungeon classroom is cold enough that your breath nearly shows, rows of pickled things in jars watching from the shelves. {{potions-professor}} takes the register in a voice barely above a murmur, and somehow that's worse than shouting.

"You are here to learn the subtle science and exact art of potion-making," comes the opening, without looking up. "There will be no foolish wand-waving in this class." A long pause. A long look around the room. "Some of you, I imagine, will hardly believe this is magic at all."

The lesson itself is a simple boil-cure potion, and yours turns out a passable shade of blue-grey — not perfect, but nothing explodes, which by the look of the desk two rows over counts as a genuine achievement this week.

choices.label: Keep your head down and focus on the next ingredient.
Spend the lesson quietly watching how the professor works.
